page.content: The advantages of natural homing ability and specific transfer of bioactive molecules (152, 153) are contrasted by the disadvantages of a lack of standardization of pre-analytical variables (127, 140). Highly hydrophobic drugs and hydrophilic drugs can be packaged (152, 154), but there is no clear nomenclature leading to variable qualitative and quantitative analysis (1, 155). A good delivery vehicle for drugs based on the ability to cross the blood–brain barrier (153, 156) faces the disadvantage of no recommended isolation protocol for clinical grade production and quality control of EV-based therapeutics (68, 142).

drugs can be packaged (152, 154), but there is no clear nomenclature leading to variable qualitative and quantitative analysis (1,
155). A good delivery vehicle for drugs based on the ability to cross the blood–brain barrier (153, 156) faces the disadvantage of
no recommended isolation protocol for clinical grade production and quality control of EV-based therapeutics (68, 142).

page.content: It is easy to adapt/optimize content and surface receptors for site-specific delivery (147, 154, 156), yet comprised of heterogeneous components depending on the isolation, handling, and agonist(s) used (67, 69, 128–130). More stable upon freezing and thawing compared with cells, biocompatible, and non-cytotoxic (142, 156), there remains a need to establish techniques and methodologies to rigorously quantify and characterize the molecular and physical aspects of EVs (142). In dietary studies, Denmark (2000–2002) reported a national dietary survey with N: F: 1,307; M: 1,156 aged 25–64 years using a 7-day pre-coded food record (25), while Finland (2002) used a national dietary survey (FINDIET) with N: F: 1,095; M: 912 aged 25–64 years via 48-h dietary recall (26). Italy (1994–1996) conducted a national dietary survey with N: F: 682; M: 586 aged 25–64 years employing a 7-day mixed survey technique (27), Sweden (1987–1990) performed cohort studies (Swedish Mammography Cohort (SMC) & Cohort of Swedish Men (COSM)) with N: F: 37,854; M: 45,906 aged 45–79 years using a 96-item food frequency questionnaire (FFQ) (28), and the United Kingdom (2000–2001) executed a national dietary survey with N: F: 958; M: 766 aged 19–64 years utilizing a 7-day dietary record (29).

heterogeneous components depending on the isolation, handling, and agonist(s) used (67, 69, 128–130). More stable upon
freezing and thawing compared with cells, biocompatible, and non-cytotoxic (142, 156), there remains a need to establish
techniques and methodologies to rigorously quantify and characterize the molecular and physical aspects of EVs (142). In
dietary studies, Denmark (2000–2002) reported a national dietary survey with N: F: 1,307; M: 1,156 aged 25–64 years using a
7-day pre-coded food record (25), while Finland (2002) used a national dietary survey (FINDIET) with N: F: 1,095; M: 912
aged 25–64 years via 48-h dietary recall (26). Italy (1994–1996) conducted a national dietary survey with N: F: 682; M: 586
aged 25–64 years employing a 7-day mixed survey technique (27), Sweden (1987–1990) performed cohort studies (Swedish
Mammography Cohort (SMC) & Cohort of Swedish Men (COSM)) with N: F: 37,854; M: 45,906 aged 45–79 years using a 96-
item food frequency questionnaire (FFQ) (28), and the United Kingdom (2000–2001) executed a national dietary survey with
N: F: 958; M: 766 aged 19–64 years utilizing a 7-day dietary record (29).
